title: parodia rutilans subsp. veeniana
intro: Parodia rutilans subsp. veeniana, commonly known as Veen's Ball or Veen's Parodia, is a rare subspecies of the Parodia rutilans cactus, native to Brazil and known for its striking, vibrant yellow flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Parodia rutilans subsp. veeniana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also sometimes used in herbal tinctures and capsules, although its availability is limited due to its rarity.
